Clientèle de Stylish® au 1-855-789-5352

le fichier est disponible sous chaque modèle d'évier.
Respectez tous les règlements de plomberie et de
construction conformément aux exigences de votre
État.
Il n'est pas recommandé de l'installer avec un comptoir
en stratifié.

Placez le gabarit de découpe dans la position
souhaitée sur le comptoir, en plaçant le centre de la
cuvette au-dessus du meuble.
Remarque: l'installation de l'évier est normalement
effectuée par le fournisseur du comptoir.
Tracez le contour du gabarit et découpez le comptoir
selon le contour du gabarit en utilisant les outils de
coupe appropriés. Le modèle est donné à titre indicatif
seulement. Utilisez un évier pour assurer une ouverture
précise de la découpe.
Il est recommandé d'utiliser les dimensions réelles de
l'évier pour assurer la découpe la plus précise possible.
Le dessin ou le gabarit sert de référence.

est lisse et exempte de débris. Appliquez un cordon
continu de mastic silicone de manière uniforme sur le

Vérifiez à nouveau l'alignement de l'évier et resserrez les
attaches pour sécuriser l'évier. Essuyez l'excès de mastic
silicone avec un chiffon humide. Raccordez
l'évacuation et l'alimentation en eau conformément
aux instructions du fabricant.
Remarque: si l'évier est installé selon l'option 2, il faudra
prévoir des moyens spéciaux pour le maintenir en place
pendant que la silicone adhésive durcit.
Percez des trous sur la face inférieure du comptoir, à
environ 1/2 po. du bord de l'évier à installer. Les trous
doivent être régulièrement espacés d'environ 6 à 8
pouces à partir de chaque coin, comme indiqué

con un martillo hasta quedar al ras.

drilled holes, so they are ready to fasten the sink flange.

damp cloth. Connect drain and water supply

title: s_627wk_stylish_undermount_installation_manual_english_and_french (6/11)
body:
Utilisez des ciseaux pour couper sur la ligne du modèle
de papier correspondant au type de révélation que
vous souhaitez (révélation négative, positive ou
affleurante)
3/8" Positive 3/8" Positive
1/8" Négative
1/8" Négative
Affleurante
Affleurante
Placez le gabarit de découpe dans la position
souhaitée sur le comptoir, en plaçant le centre de la
cuvette au-dessus du meuble.
Remarque: l'installation de l'évier est normalement
effectuée par le fournisseur du comptoir.
Tracez le contour du gabarit et découpez le comptoir
selon le contour du gabarit en utilisant les outils de
coupe appropriés. Le modèle est donné à titre indicatif
seulement. Utilisez un évier pour assurer une ouverture
précise de la découpe.
Il est recommandé d'utiliser les dimensions réelles de
l'évier pour assurer la découpe la plus précise possible.

the original appearance of your sink. Ensure that you thoroughly
rinse your sink and wipe it dry. We recommend the use of a stainless
steel grid that fits inside the bottom of the sink bowl and protect
the sink from scratches.

title: s_642wk_stylish_undermount_installation_manual_english (2/4)
body:
IF YOU HAVE PURCHASED A UNDERMOUNT SINK, PLEASE MAKE SURE TO READ THESE INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY
INSTALLATION GUIDE
UNDERMOUNT
STAINLESS STEEL KITCHEN SINK
WARRANTY
1
Place cut-out template in desired position
on countertop locating center of the bowl over cabinet.
Note: Sink installation is normally
performed by the countertop provider.
Trace the outline of the template and cut countertop
according to templated outline using appropriate
cutting tools. Template is for guide only. Use physical sink
to ensure accurate cut-out opening.
It is recommended to use the actual sink
dimensions to ensure the most accurate cut out.
Drawing or template are for reference.
Use scissors to cut along the line of the paper template
for you desired reveal type (Negative, Positive or Flush
Reveal)
3/8" Positive Reveal 3/8" Positive
1/8" Negative
1/8" Negative overhanging
Flush/Zero
Prior to installation:
Make sure that you have all necessary parts by
checking the parts list. If any part is missing, please
contact Stylish® Customer Service at +1-855-789-5352 for
a replacement.
Inspect the sink prior to installation to make sure that it
has not been damaged during shipping.
You may use the cut-out template provided or
download DXF file from www.stylishkb.com, the file is
available under each sink model.
Observe all plumbing and building codes according to
your state requirements.

CARE AND CLEANING
5
Drill holes on the underside of the countertop,
approximately 1/2" from edge of the sink to be installed.
Holes should be evenly spaced approximately 6" to 8"
apart starting from every corner as shown below.
Option 2. Alternatively wood studs combined with
silicone caulk can be used to hold sink against
countertop without using mounting brackets.
Option 1. Insert anchors under the countertop on the
drilled holes, so they are ready to fasten the sink flange.
Anchors should be inserted with a hammer until flush.
Check alignment of sink again and tighten fasteners
to secure sink. Wipe away excess silicone caulk with
damp cloth. Connect drain and water supply
according to manufacturer’s instruction.

Clean your sink every day to prevent a buildup of soap

title: p_220d_spec_sheet_eng (2/2)
body:
scum, grease and dirt. Use a wet sponge with liquid
dishwashing. Scrub the sink's surface using a soft non-abra-
sive cloth or sponge. Wipe the sink dry with a dish towel or
other soft cloth to eliminate water stains. For deep clean
cover the sink with paper or cloth towels and pour undilu-
ted bleach. Be sure the area is well ventilated and let the
bleach-saturated towels sit for 15 to 30 minutes. Rinse
thoroughly with water and wipe dry with a dish towel or
other soft cloth.
Our porcelain sinks are made
from mixture of clay and other
minerals, that is triple-glazed
and triple-fire at about 2.000
°F. The result is a glossy
porcelain surface known as
vitreous china, which is denser,
glossier and more durable
than regular porcelain.

Premium 16 gauge stainless
steel with 18/10 chrome-nickel
content. The 18% of chrome
content provides durability,

Rinse and dry your sink with a clean soft cloth, working with, not across,
the grain. We recommend the use of a mild solution of water and vinegar
to remove any mineral deposits that may have built up on your sink.
Use mild non-abrasive liquid cream cleaners that do not contain
chlorine, sodium hypochlorite or hydrochloric acid to help maintain the
original appearance of your sink. Ensure that you thoroughly rinse your
sink and wipe it dry. We recommend the use of a stainless steel grid that
fits inside the bottom of the sink bowl and protect the sink from scratches.

Clean your sink every day to prevent a buildup of soap
scum, grease and dirt. Use a wet sponge with liquid
dishwashing. Scrub the sink's surface using a soft non-abra-

title: p_220_spec_sheet_eng (2/2)
body:
sive cloth or sponge. Wipe the sink dry with a dish towel or
other soft cloth to eliminate water stains. For deep clean
cover the sink with paper or cloth towels and pour undilu-
ted bleach. Be sure the area is well ventilated and let the
bleach-saturated towels sit for 15 to 30 minutes. Rinse
thoroughly with water and wipe dry with a dish towel or
other soft cloth.
Our porcelain sinks are made
from mixture of clay and other
minerals, that is triple-glazed
and triple-fire at about 2.000
°F. The result is a glossy
porcelain surface known as
vitreous china, which is denser,
glossier and more durable
than regular porcelain.

IF YOU HAVE PURCHASED A UNDERMOUNT SINK, PLEASE MAKE SURE TO READ THESE INSTRUCTIONS CAREFULLY
INSTALLATION GUIDE
UNDERMOUNT
STAINLESS STEEL KITCHEN SINK
WARRANTY
1
Place cut-out template in desired position
on countertop locating center of the bowl over cabinet.
Note: Sink installation is normally
performed by the countertop provider.
Trace the outline of the template and cut countertop
according to templated outline using appropriate
cutting tools. Template is for guide only. Use physical sink
to ensure accurate cut-out opening.
It is recommended to use the actual sink
dimensions to ensure the most accurate cut out.
Drawing or template are for reference.
Use scissors to cut along the line of the paper template
for you desired reveal type (Negative, Positive or Flush
Reveal)
3/8" Positive Reveal 3/8" Positive
1/8" Negative
1/8" Negative overhanging
Flush/Zero
Prior to installation:
Make sure that you have all necessary parts by
checking the parts list. If any part is missing, please
contact Stylish® Customer Service at +1-855-789-5352 for
a replacement.
Inspect the sink prior to installation to make sure that it
has not been damaged during shipping.
You may use the cut-out template provided or
download DXF file from www.stylishkb.com, the file is
available under each sink model.
Observe all plumbing and building codes according to
your state requirements.

according to manufacturer’s instruction.

Place cut-out template in desired position

Note: Sink installation is normally
performed by the countertop provider.
Trace the outline of the template and cut countertop
according to templated outline using appropriate
cutting tools. Template is for guide only. Use physical sink
to ensure accurate cut-out opening.

dimensions to ensure the most accurate cut out.

checking the parts list. If any part is missing, please
contact Stylish® Customer Service at +1-855-789-5352 for
a replacement.
Inspect the sink prior to installation to make sure that it
has not been damaged during shipping.
You may use the cut-out template provided or
download DXF file from www.stylishkb.com, the file is
available under each sink model.
Observe all plumbing and building codes according to
your state requirements.

INSTALLATION GUIDE
UNDERMOUNT
STAINLESS STEEL KITCHEN SINK
WARRANTY
1
Place cut-out template in desired position
on countertop locating center of the bowl over cabinet.
Note: Sink installation is normally
performed by the countertop provider.
Trace the outline of the template and cut countertop
according to templated outline using appropriate
cutting tools. Template is for guide only. Use physical sink
to ensure accurate cut-out opening.
It is recommended to use the actual sink
dimensions to ensure the most accurate cut out.
Drawing or template are for reference.
Use scissors to cut along the line of the paper template
for you desired reveal type (Negative, Positive or Flush
Reveal)
3/8" Positive Reveal 3/8" Positive
1/8" Negative
1/8" Negative overhanging
Flush/Zero
Prior to installation:
Make sure that you have all necessary parts by
checking the parts list. If any part is missing, please
contact Stylish® Customer Service at +1-855-789-5352 for
a replacement.
Inspect the sink prior to installation to make sure that it
has not been damaged during shipping.
You may use the cut-out template provided or
download DXF file from www.stylishkb.com, the file is
available under each sink model.
Observe all plumbing and building codes according to
your state requirements.

original appearance of your sink. Ensure that you thoroughly rinse your
sink and wipe it dry. We recommend the use of a stainless steel grid that
fits inside the bottom of the sink bowl and protect the sink from scratches.

Placez le gabarit de découpe dans la position
souhaitée sur le comptoir, en plaçant le centre de la
cuvette au-dessus du meuble.
Remarque: l'installation de l'évier est normalement
effectuée par le fournisseur du comptoir.
Tracez le contour du gabarit et découpez le comptoir
selon le contour du gabarit en utilisant les outils de
coupe appropriés. Le modèle est donné à titre indicatif
seulement. Utilisez un évier pour assurer une ouverture
précise de la découpe.
Il est recommandé d'utiliser les dimensions réelles de
l'évier pour assurer la découpe la plus précise possible.
Le dessin ou le gabarit sert de référence.

3. Avec une éponge, frottez toutes les parois de l'évier

Nettoyez le rebord de l'évier et le dessous de la zone
découpée du comptoir, en vous assurant que la zone
est lisse et exempte de débris. Appliquez un cordon
continu de mastic silicone de manière uniforme sur le
dessus de la bordure de l'évier.
Vérifiez à nouveau l'alignement de l'évier et resserrez les
attaches pour sécuriser l'évier. Essuyez l'excès de mastic
silicone avec un chiffon humide. Raccordez
l'évacuation et l'alimentation en eau conformément
aux instructions du fabricant.
Remarque: si l'évier est installé selon l'option 2, il faudra
prévoir des moyens spéciaux pour le maintenir en place
pendant que la silicone adhésive durcit.
Percez des trous sur la face inférieure du comptoir, à
environ 1/2 po. du bord de l'évier à installer. Les trous
doivent être régulièrement espacés d'environ 6 à 8
pouces à partir de chaque coin, comme indiqué

Place cut-out template in desired position
on countertop locating center of the bowl over cabinet.

performed by the countertop provider.

according to templated outline using appropriate
cutting tools. Template is for guide only. Use physical sink
to ensure accurate cut-out opening.

dimensions to ensure the most accurate cut out.

contact Stylish® Customer Service at +1-855-789-5352 for
a replacement.
Inspect the sink prior to installation to make sure that it
has not been damaged during shipping.
You may use the cut-out template provided or
download DXF file from www.stylishkb.com, the file is
available under each sink model.
Observe all plumbing and building codes according to
your state requirements.

Placez le gabarit de découpe dans la position
souhaitée sur le comptoir, en plaçant le centre de la
cuvette au-dessus du meuble.
Remarque: l'installation de l'évier est normalement
effectuée par le fournisseur du comptoir.
Tracez le contour du gabarit et découpez le comptoir
selon le contour du gabarit en utilisant les outils de
coupe appropriés. Le modèle est donné à titre indicatif
seulement. Utilisez un évier pour assurer une ouverture
précise de la découpe.
Il est recommandé d'utiliser les dimensions réelles de
l'évier pour assurer la découpe la plus précise possible.
Le dessin ou le gabarit sert de référence.

3. Avec une éponge, frottez toutes les parois de l'évier

WARRANTY

Place cut-out template in desired position
on countertop locating center of the bowl over cabinet.
Note: Sink installation is normally
performed by the countertop provider.
Trace the outline of the template and cut countertop
according to templated outline using appropriate
cutting tools. Template is for guide only. Use physical sink
to ensure accurate cut-out opening.

dimensions to ensure the most accurate cut out.

Inspect the sink prior to installation to make sure that it
has not been damaged during shipping.

available under each sink model.
Observe all plumbing and building codes according to
your state requirements.

has not been damaged during shipping.

available under each sink model.
Observe all plumbing and building codes according to
your state requirements.

Rinse and dry your sink with a clean soft cloth, working with, not a

and vinegar to remove any mineral deposits that may have built

Use mild non-abrasive liquid cream cleaners that do not contain
chlorine, sodium hypochlorite or hydrochloric acid to help maintain
the original appearance of your sink. Ensure that you thoroughly
rinse your sink and wipe it dry. We recommend the use of a stainless
steel grid that fits inside the bottom of the sink bowl and protect
the sink from scratches.

To clean a Stainless Steel colander:
1.Rinse the colander with water.
2. Coat the colander with baking soda, Flitz or Barkeepers friend.
3. With sponge, scrub all colander walls in the direction of the
steel grain.
4. Spray on undiluted vinegar.
5. Rinse again.
6. Buff with a few drops of olive oil.
7. If necessary, repeat procedure.

title: $20 Shipping Fees
body:
Scenario: $20 Shipping Fees
We provide a comprehensive warranty covering factory defects or components' durability not meeting expectations.
Under our warranty, we offer free replacement spare parts. However, please note that shipping costs are not included. You have the option to pick up the parts at our Warehouse in Cambridge, Ontario, or we can ship them to you for a small fee of $20. Kindly inform us of your preferred method.
To proceed, please visit www.Stylishkb.com and scroll down to make a payment. Once completed, please send a screenshot of the receipt, and we will promptly ship the new part to you.

title: $40 Replacement Part plus Shipping Fees`
body:
Scenario: $40 Replacement Part plus Shipping Fees
We offer a comprehensive warranty that covers factory defects and issues related to the durability of components not meeting expected standards.
Under this warranty, we provide free replacement parts. However, as an invoice cannot be provided at this time, the warranty has been voided, and the replacement part is no longer eligible for coverage.
Please note that shipping costs are not included. You may choose to pick up the part at our warehouse in Cambridge, Ontario, or have it shipped to you. The total cost for the part and shipping is $40.
To proceed, please visit www.Stylishkb.com and scroll down to make a payment. Once completed, please send a screenshot of the receipt, and we will promptly ship the new part to you.

title: Asking for Warranty
body:
Scenario: Asking for Warranty
We apologize for the inconvenience. Please send the information below to verify your warranty:

title: Bent Sink 1 (1/2)
body:
Scenario: Bent Sink 1
We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience you've experienced. This situation is quite uncommon and appears to be the result of rough handling by the courier during transit. While the box itself may have arrived undamaged, it's clear the item sustained some impact.
Due to the size and weight of our sinks, courier services can sometimes handle packages more roughly than intended. While our packaging includes protective foam and reinforced cardboard, it unfortunately wasn't enough to fully prevent the bends in this case.
The good news:
The sink itself remains fully functional for undermount installation. The bends appear limited to the flange, which will not be visible once installed. That said, it's essential to ensure the sink sits level during installation to prevent water seepage between the countertop and sink.
Here's what we recommend:
Straightening the Flange:
Option 1: Manual Correction
Place the sink upside down on a soft surface (e.g., carpet or towel).
Gently tap the bent flange using a rubber mallet or hammer with a protective cloth.
Use a level or ruler to confirm the flange is flat and gaps are under 1/16", which is sufficient for proper silicone sealing.
Option 2: Pressure Correction During Install
Use clamps and wooden shims or studs between the flange and countertop during installation to apply pressure and ensure a flush fit.

There is no need to call a plumber - the hose is very easy to replace on your own. Please follow the updated installation instructions below:
How to Replace a Pull-Down Faucet Hose
Turn off the water supply under the sink.
Disconnect the old hose from the faucet quickconnector, remove the weight and the spout head.
Pull the old hose out through the faucet spout.
Feed the new hose through the spout and down under the sink.
Reconnect the hose to the faucet quickconnector, reattach the weight and the spout head.
Turn the water back on and test for proper operation and leaks.
Tip: Hand-tighten connections, then snug gently if needed-do not overtighten.
If you need any help while installing the handle, feel free to reach out - we're always happy to assist!

title: Cartridge Leak Issue
body:
Scenario: Cartridge Leak Issue
Thanks for reaching out. Based on your issue, it may be related to the faucet cartridge. Below are quick steps to check it:
How to Check the Cartridge:
Turn off water supply under the sink.
Remove the handle using an Allen key or screwdriver.
Unscrew the retaining nut and pull out the cartridge.
Inspect for damage, debris, or worn seals.
Clean (if reusing) with vinegar and re-lubricate O-rings.
Reinstall cartridge and handle.
Turn water back on and test.
If you see any damage or the issue persists, let us know and we'll send a replacement cartridge under warranty.
Feel free to send photos or a short video if you'd like us to review.

title: Clean Spout Head
body:
Scenario: Clean Spout Head
Thank you for providing the requested information.
The issue you're experiencing is often caused by mineral buildup or installation debris, and a thorough cleaning usually resolves it. Please follow the steps below to clean your faucet aerator:
Remove the spout head by unscrewing it from the faucet.
Soak the spout head in a 50/50 vinegar and water solution for 24 hours to help dissolve any internal calcification.
- For faster results, you can use a 50/50 solution of CLR and water and soak the spout head for 10 minutes instead.
- After soaking, gently scrub off any remaining hard water deposits using a toothbrush. We recommend wearing gloves during this process.
If necessary, unscrew the aerator from the spout head and clean it separately using the same solution and a toothbrush.
Rinse all parts thoroughly, reassemble the components, and test the faucet.
?? Important: Do not fully tighten the aerator, as this can cause the button mechanism to malfunction.
Once you've completed these steps, please let us know how it goes. We're here to help and will gladly provide further assistance if needed.

title: Cleaning Pietra Sinks
body:
Scenario: Cleaning Pietra Sinks
I hope this email finds you well.
Composite granite sinks are molded products, and as such, some minor surface imperfections can occur. Fortunately, these marks are always easy to remove with the right approach.
We recommend using a Scotch-Brite scouring sponge in combination with Bar Keepers Friend, applying firm, even strokes to the affected area. This method has proven effective in removing even stubborn marks, such as permanent marker or (e.g., Sharpie), from composite granite surfaces.
Please avoid using bleach or other harsh chemicals, as these can damage or degrade the finish of the sink over time.
Check the following link to purchase it online: https://www.homedepot.ca/product/bar-keepers-friend-750ml-soft-multi-surface-cleanser/1001014033
You can also follow the steps on the following video for better results: https://youtu.be/2J97z5Lg0s0?si=E1nQr6MTQuCHZSwC
If you have any further questions or need additional assistance, feel free to reach out.

title: Cleaning Steel Sink 1
body:
Scenario: Cleaning Steel Sink 1
Stylish® Stainless Steel sinks are crafted from high-quality chrome-nickel steel, providing exceptional strength and durability. This material is highly resistant to staining, rust, and corrosion, and is designed not to chip, craze, flake, or break.
Discoloration and most marks on stainless steel are caused by water-borne deposits clinging to its surface. They are always the result of an outside source rather than coming from the steel itself.
We recommend using BAR KEEPERS FRIEND, use the link below to purchase the cleaning solution:
https://www.homedepot.ca/product/bar-keepers-friend-750ml-soft-multi-surface-cleanser/1001014033
Daily Sink Care:
- Rinse and dry with a soft cloth, following the grain to avoid spots.
- Use mild, non-abrasive cleaners. Products like 'Flitz' or 'Bar Keepers Friend' are good choices. Dry the sink thoroughly after cleaning.
Avoid:
- Abrasive sponges, wire brushes, or steel wool, which can scratch and dull the sink.
- Leaving salt, vinegar, citric juices, or strong acids on the sink, which can cause corrosion.
- Abrasive cleaners or bleach, which can damage the finish.
- Defrosting food directly on the sink; use a plate or pot instead.
- Leaving wet sponges or cloths on the sink, as they can discolor the surface.
See the instructions on the following video for better results: https://youtu.be/hzyjEecX1pw?si=6RIODFRpk7_d-eh1

title: Cleaning Steel Sink 2
body:
Scenario: Cleaning Steel Sink 2
We understand your concern about rust on your stainless steel sink and want to assure you that we'll take care of it.
Rust stains are rare and typically result from external sources like water, cookware, or iron particles-not a fault in the sink itself. Even high-end stainless steel products can experience this.
The good news is these stains are easy to remove. Cleaners like Bar Keepers Friend or Autosol Shine Cream work well. Just rinse thoroughly and dry the sink after cleaning to help prevent recurrence.
We'll be sending you a complimentary cleaning kit, including a powerful cleaning cream and sponge, along with a video guide.
Please send us your full name and shipping address so we can send it out right away. Let us know if you need any further assistance-we're here to help.

title: LODI Stream Issue
body:
Scenario: LODI Stream Issue
Thanks for reaching out, and we apologize for the inconvenience. It sounds like the issue may be coming from inside the spout head. In these cases, we recommend the following two troubleshooting steps:
Remove debris manually:
Insert the tip of a screwdriver into the spout head and make circular movements. This can help break up and remove any installation debris that may have been left inside by the plumber.
Clear hard buildup:
If the first method doesn't resolve the issue, there may be a hard buildup inside the spout. In this case, we recommend carefully using a 5mm drill bit to break through the blockage.
Please let us know if you'd like further assistance or have any questions before starting.

It's also possible that a family member may have opened the box and unintentionally misplaced the grids.
If possible, could you kindly double-check with anyone who may have had access to the sink prior to installation? The grids are always packed with care, and it is highly unlikely that they would be missing from the box.

title: Slow water flow
body:
Scenario: Slow water flow
We apologize for the inconvenience. We would like to know if the issue occurred suddenly, or if the flow had been gradually slowing down over time.
To troubleshoot this issue and determine the cause of the slow water flow, please follow these steps:
Remove the spout head (it can be done by hand) and check the water flow. Video Guide: https://youtu.be/KZ6dPWnPjwc
Check your water supply shut-off valves-are they fully open? If they are, proceed to step 3.
Inspect underneath the faucet to ensure none of the hoses are kinked, twisted, or tangled. Check if the pull-out hose moves smoothly. Kinked or twisted hoses can cause a slow flow.
Verify that the water supply lines from your home plumbing have good water flow and pressure.
Check whether the slow flow occurs with both hot and cold water, or if it's specific to one.
If none of the above are causing the issue, the slow flow could be due to a clogged cartridge. Let us know how it goes or if you need further assistance to determine the best course of action.

title: Wood Studs - Option 2
body:
Scenario: Wood Studs - Option 2
Please keep in mind that there are two mounting systems available for this sink: using brackets or wood studs. We recommend the wood studs option, as it eliminates the need to drill holes into your valuable and expensive countertop, reducing the risk of damage or even cracking the entire surface.
Using wood studs is a safer method since the installer applies high-quality silicone caulk, providing a secure hold that is just as effective as using clips.
Would you still prefer to proceed with the clips? Please let us know your preference.
